subject: Re: Subtomogram handedness
body:
the volume flipping flag for AreTomo does not have any impact on the handedness of the volume generated by warp 
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On May 8, 2024, at 15:18, 정민규 <jmg0910@snu.ac.kr> wrote:
﻿
Hi Alister
Thank you for your quick response! I used aretomo for tilt-series alignment and I performed -flipvol option. 
If I understand correctly, this could be the problem,  is it right?
Best,
Mingyu
2024년 5월 8일 (수) 오후 11:01, Alister Burt <
alisterburt@gmail.com
>님이 작성:
Hi Mingyu,
What you checked was defocus handedness - a parameter of the CTF model in tomograms which tells you how defocus changes across the tilt axis for a particular tilt angle.
This is completely decoupled from physical handedness and there can be various things which make physical handedness wrong in tomograms
- 2D images flipped by some processing step/acquisition
- tilt axis angle off by 180
- flip along YZ after reconstruction in IMOD (not relevant for Warp)
If you know that the handedness in your tomograms is wrong and know that it being correct is important for your spatial analyses you need to account for this when interpreting the data that comes out of your refinements :)
Cheers,
Alister
On Wed, 8 May 2024 at 14:31, Mingyu Jung <
jmg0910@snu.ac.kr
> wrote:
Hi all,
I performed Warp-Relion-M pipeline for subtomogram averaging. I checked and confirmed the handedness of tilt series in Warp. But the averaged map from relion have flipped structure. Is it normal? It is an  important issue to analyze the orientational information from starfile. 
Thank you
Best,
Mingyu
